commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=58b1e7543b484b14dabf7530572c76c6d56d06fa)
commands.review_summary:
The PR removes the built-in `google-generative-ai` tagged fallback, overrides bundled Google Gemini provider hooks to return native reasoning output, and updates provider utility and Google hook tests.

PR surface: Source -11, Tests 0. Total -11 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main resolves Google/Gemini through tagged reasoning while native Google requests include `thinkingConfig.includeThoughts`, matching the linked issue. I did not run a paid live Gemini loop in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] A narrow automated repair can make the reasoning hook conditional and add sibling-provider regression coverage before re-review.

target.title: fix(providers): use native reasoning mode for Gemini instead of tagged
target.body:
## Summary

Gemini 2.5 Pro/Flash exposes reasoning via native `thinkingParts` through `thinkingConfig.includeThoughts`. The `google-gemini` family hook in `GOOGLE_GEMINI_PROVIDER_HOOKS` was unconditionally returning `"tagged"`, which injects a `<think>…</think>/<final>…</final>` directive into the system prompt at the same time as the native thinking config is active.

The model then:
1. Emits a native `thought:true` part
2. Opens a `<think>` text block per the injected directive
3. Pivots to a tool call before closing `</think>`
4. Returns an empty post-tool assistant turn (`content: []`)

Gateway logs: `incomplete turn detected: stopReason=stop payloads=0` — surfacing a spurious error to the user, while burning ~140K input tokens.

### Changes

- **`extensions/google/provider-hooks.ts`** — override `resolveReasoningOutputMode` in `GOOGLE_GEMINI_PROVIDER_HOOKS` to return `"native"`, superseding the `google-gemini` family default of `"tagged"`
- **`src/utils/provider-utils.ts`** — remove the now-dead `BUILTIN_REASONING_OUTPUT_MODES` entry keyed on `"google-generative-ai"`: all production callers pass the plugin id `"google"` (never the transport API string), so the map was unreachable; the plugin hook was always the effective path
- Tests updated in both `provider-utils.test.ts` and `extensions/google/index.test.ts`

## Verification

**Real behavior proof**

- `Behavior addressed`: Gemini 2.5 Pro/Flash with `thinkingDefault: medium` producing `payloads=0` errors and spurious user-facing error messages on turns that include a tool call, as reported in #69220
- `Real environment tested`: macOS arm64, Node v24.15.0, local source checkout
- `Exact steps or command run after this patch`: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/utils/provider-utils.test.ts` (25/25 passed); `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/google/index.test.ts` (8/8 passed); `node ./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check` (clean); `tsgo -p tsconfig.core.json` (exit 0)
- `Evidence after fix`: `isReasoningTagProvider("google", ...)` now returns `false`; the `<think>/<final>` system-prompt directive is no longer injected for Gemini; native `thinkingParts` are processed without conflict
- `Observed result after fix`: all touched tests green, no format/lint/type errors
- `What was not tested`: live Gemini 2.5 Pro/Flash end-to-end run confirming absence of `payloads=0` error (requires live API key + Crabbox); older Gemini models without native thinking (expected unaffected — `"native"` mode simply means core does not inject text-tag reasoning directives)

Fixes #69220

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
